reviews.review_description:
It's one of the few games that uses hand tracking as a genuinely useful control method rather than just a gimmick, which helps it stand out from other games in the genre.
The gameplay is a casual roguelike shooter with plenty of replay value thanks to unlockable characters, perks, weapons, attachments, bosses, and game modes. Definitely worth trying and worth the price.
The game feels more futuristic than realistic. The logic of button placement is complex and unclear. These aspects should be simplified, because the game is not bad — it can definitely be improved.
i was definitely skeptical the hand controls and it took a little bit to get used to them but after i got used to them it was actually pretty intuitive and really fun to play

When I saw this game was controlled completely with your hands I was skeptical, the only expierences I had with that kind of gameplay was from a couple of half baked vr games

However this wasnt that 

The tutorial is extremely well made teaching you how to adapt to this new control style quite well, the gestures are simple which for a game like this work perfectly, nearly halfway throuvh my first run i had basically forgotten i wasn't using controllers (although that may just be a me thing) the gun play is extremely fun the arts style of this game is absolutely excellent and I love the character personalities with each of them feeling quite unique to them, additionally with the gun mods, upgrades and how many playable Characters there are this game has so much content in it. Overall I would absolutely recommend this to someone who wants a polished and very fun Vr game, this game is great and uses the medium its in very very well

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your positive and enthusiastic review of Rogue Ascent! It's always a blast to hear players' reactions to playing a unique hands-first title, especially when it defies expectations going in!

reviews.review_description: Every shot you take is a slow energy ball that has minor tracking attached to them. Hitting anything is not really reliant on skill and is not satisfying at all. Also the tutorial has comfort options on by default and its incredibly disorienting to move and see the world blur around you. And every movement is on a track and you cant move around freely. Just... Why? i didn't even get out of the tutorial because it was so uncomfortable to play

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for sharing your review of Rogue Ascent.  We recommend adjusting the comfort settings accessible from the game's main menu (accessible immediately after the tutorial); including movement speed, and blur toggle. Rogue Ascent prioritizes user experience and player comfort and we build its gameplay systems around this pillar, so we take your review seriously. We specifically design player projectiles with homing to allow players to focus on target acquisition and enemy prioritization and allow for more dense and tense firefights. Some enemies fire easily dodged projectiles, however the majority of enemies require strategic use of shielding and bullet-time features unique to each class. If you are ready to up the challenge we invite you to experiment with higher difficulty settings  (which also contributes a bonus to your leaderboard score!). We believe these to be strengths of the game's design and we hope you'll be able to return with comfortable settings to experience those features. Cheers, Jordan.

reviews.review_description: Could only play a little bit since the graphics and colors hurt the eyes. But if your able to get past that, it could be a good game to play.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback regarding the visual intensity. Rogue Ascent also features a Low Sensory Mode which we hope serves to accommodate for your discomfort. Enabling Low Sensory mode from the Settings Menu puts the following changes into effect:

Reduces Post Processing Strength (Bloom, Chromatic Aberration)
Reduces HUD Effects (Ult Indicator, Limited Perk Display, Overlays, UI Shakes)
Reduces Particles (Floating Text, Muzzle Flashes, Heal Zones, Weapon Sparkles)
Removes Select Energy Field Materials (Player Shield, Scans, Uplink, Heal Zones)
Removes Unit Floor Icons, Spinning Klaxon Lights

reviews.review_description: Played many runs of this game and it never got boring, the soundtrack perfectly matched the gameplay and hand tracking worked perfectly. Tons of different rooms and guns and characters that provide tons of replay ability. LOVE IT!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your glowing review! The soundtrack is the brilliant work of Edward Ray, who composed an amazing original soundtrack album for Rogue Ascent (also available on Spotify!) It's always great to know when we deliver on our mission to create groundbreaking, high quality gameplay experiences!

reviews.review_description: What a neat game. You have to try it with hand tracking.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your positive review of Rogue Ascent! We're always happy to exceed our goal of making hand tracking exciting and accessible!

reviews.review_description: It still could be better though

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing your review of Rogue Ascent! We appreciate the praise and the honest feedback!

reviews.review_description:
I’ve never seen such a game with such a unique style. You go through an alien spaceship climbing to the top ranks, upgrading, and fighting bosses all with your fingers! The gameplay is so satisfying too, each time I played a learned a little more which helped me get farther along. Finally crafting that weapon I wanted and destroying my enemies is so nice too

And the whole time, getting to look at all the art was just beautiful, with all the nice colors/ designs and my hands matching the special hands of the character I chose. Restarting just meant it was another adventure

Overall it’s a very nice game that I enjoyed throughout, and if it sounds good to you I think it’s worth getting

developer_response.body: Thank you for writing such high praise of Rogue Ascent and its style! It is the result of many talented and dedicated people (including players like you!) who have turned it into such a unique and satisfying experience to share!

reviews.review_description:
Has anyone actually gotten past the tutorial
Once you shoot the camera you are just left to run around and do nothing and I can’t return it WTH

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for bringing your issue to the team. The issue has been resolved and functionality has been restored.

reviews.review_description: Brilliant game  ..this  game could do with a   slow mode just walking  .but the ideas are new .the graphics on  of people  could be a lot better  . Hopefully  with up dates this  this could be the best game on oculus 3 .

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your review! We're thrilled to hear you enjoy Rogue Ascent's innovative gameplay!

reviews.review_description: Something as essential as turning your camera requires pulling up the settings menu and manually clicking a button to snap once left or once right is OBSURD. Not to mention how I couldn't even get passed the tutorial because of a glitch or something- I was stuck with a siren blaring and having nowhere to go but a tiny ship hallway. *Don't waste your time.*

developer_response.body:
Hi, the issue has been resolved and functionality has been restored. Thank you for reporting the issue to the team.

We suggest using the game's built-in Snap Turn Gesture (two thumbs in the direction to rotate). Our players who play with Snap Turn prefer this method over manual snap turning via the wrist menu.

reviews.review_description:
Thank you! It was indeed fixed in the locomotion settings. I’m leaving my original review up for awareness but will adjust my rating. I feel like this game could benefit if it had a locomotion comfort option screen on 1st boot up like so many other VR games do. Even more so because the effect is very unique to this game.

The game is fun, unique and addictive. Graphics look great and the hand tracking works flawlessly.

Why the 2 stars?

They way you move in this game is from node to node on a square grid map. Every time you switch nodes the whole screen goes blurry and pixilated until your character is in the new position. This is disorienting and immersion breaking. You would think this wouldn’t be a big deal since the action is all standing in one spot but half of the game is moving from one node to the next. Honestly it’s game breaking for me. Other people might not be as sensitive to it but this is just a warning. Playing on quest 3, I feel like this shouldn’t even be a problem.

If not for that issue the game would easily be 4 stars. Only reason I’m not refunding is because I hope it will be fixed with a patch somewhere down the line.

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for sharing your review of Rogue Ascent. If you feel at all disoriented by the locomotion blur settings you may disable them entirely in the game settings menu, along with several other visual controls! Thanks for checking out the game and we're glad you enjoy the gameplay!

reviews.review_description: I find the hand tracking confusing and uncomfortable, the constant spinning around always feels disorientating. I haven't got through the first part, there's this alarm going off, the panic ensues, I'm still trying to figure out how to move and all the wrist things and this alarm is just blaring. I don't know where I'm going or what I'm supposed to be doing, it's frustrating.

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for sharing your feedback for Rogue Ascent. If you're feeling disoriented by turning in your playspace you may take advantage of the game's Snap Turning gesture feature which helps mitigate playspace movement. If there are any mechanics or systems you're unclear about we recommend taking a look at the in-game Knowledge Base, accessible via the main menu, or practice using the Sandbox gamemode. Rogue Ascent offers various difficulty settings to help you find a pace that works for you as you learn. As always, its important to remember that hand tracking works best in a well lit environment with sleeves rolled up and wrist accessories removed. If you have any further issues or would like any one-on-one help, you can get in touch via the official Rogue Ascent Discord. Cheers!

reviews.review_description: So, I realize the devs put a lot of effort into making this a totally functional hand tracking controlled game.  The thing is, the game is actually neat, but I find a controller way easier and enjoyable to use than gesturing.  So my suggestion would be to add an option for controllers if players really wanna use them.  I think it would make the game a little more fun for some of us.  Neat concept and good basis though.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your review. We'd like to remind you that Rogue Ascent features full controller support as an alternative to its innovative use of hand-tracking. As always, if you have any issues getting your input devices working we welcome you to visit us on Discord and we'll provide you with one-on-one support!

reviews.review_description:
Amazing game and hand tracking

Take some time to get use to it but the hand tracking is pretty good

Too bad we can’t close the game without loosing the progress…

Or there is something I didn’t understand…

developer_response.body: This is a feature we are looking at adding

reviews.review_description: Now before I start, the hand tracking isn’t great which is the worst part of this game. But other than that, this game is amazing. It’s a really fun and unique game that is genuinely challenging at times. I highly recommend it. It utilizes hand tracking to its fullest and has a lot of strategy and fun choices to it.

reviews.review_title: Interesting game! But is it a must buy?
reviews.review_description:
For the experienced vr users that have played lots of games before and know the basics of vr, i would highly recommend this game.

I have realised now it is hard to find quality cheap (ish) games to occupy yourself with. Then i bought this game, it’s creative, smooth and awesome to play, especially when you are out of games to play.

Only pet peeves are the smaller facts that this game is still in development and i sometimes experience some frame drops and quality issues.

Replayability: 9/10 (obviously)
Visuals: 7/10 (stylistic but not the greatest)
Gameplay: 8/10 (gets slightly repetitive)
Fun: 8/10 (very nice and well made game)

Overall, 
8/10 would recommend for vr users looking for a fun and unique experience

developer_response.body: We're happy to hear that your experience playing Rogue Ascent has been positive!  We're excited to hear what you think of the content which has been added since your initial review during Beta. We're dedicated to ensuring the game runs smoothly across devices and have since made significant improvement to the game's overall performance. Thank you for your review of Rogue Ascent!

reviews.review_description:
One of the more fun rogue lites I have tried so far in VR. Focus on getting in doing objectives fast and getting the hell out kinda espionage like. On higher diffculities the number of enemies at once in a level and shooting at you can feel a bit too overwhelmingly/(impossible to deal with) at first. 

Very impressive amount of content here for a rogue lite fps game let alone one for €10 now. Weaving between shooting physicaly dodging bullets and stationary shield and moving back and foward to nodes ads a great sense of urgency to gameplay. Great class variety too and fun items/buffs which I feel a lot of flatscreen and VR roguelites drop the ball a bit and just give you useless 5% extra attack damage that barely stacks. 

Here you get stuff like getting burned or set on fire boosts your damage, get posioned and your attacks are posion attacks for a little bit, reloading boosts your attack damage, critical hits slow down time etc and so on. Actualy unique fun buffs that are more then just small perctange damage increase and nothing else.

Pretry good class variery and a lovely art style and soundtrack and minimal motion sickness and easy unique but also intutive controls!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing such a detailed and thorough review of Rogue Ascent! I've enjoyed sharing our inspirations together on Discord and discussing the game's development. Cheers, Jordan.
